data.title: implementation-plan skill not returning control to sprint orchestrator after completion
data.description:
## Observed Behavior

During sprint execution of epic 6863-7b38 (Figma design collaboration), the `/dso:implementation-plan` skill was invoked for each story in the layer loop. After each invocation emitted STATUS:complete, the sprint orchestrator did NOT automatically continue to the next story — it stopped instead of looping to the next layer/story.

This caused the user to have to manually invoke `/dso:implementation-plan` for each story (b4c1-06cf, 147a-5eec, 06c3-d6c7, 5250-e85b) via explicit Skill tool calls, rather than the sprint orchestrator handling the loop autonomously.

## Context at Time of Bug

- Sprint orchestrator was in Phase 2 Step 2, processing Layer loop for epic 6863-7b38
- Stories being planned: Layers 0-5 (6 stories)
- Stories requiring implementation-plan: b4c1-06cf (L0), 147a-5eec (L2), 06c3-d6c7 (L3), 5250-e85b (L4)
- After each /dso:implementation-plan invocation, the sprint orchestrator failed to emit SKILL_RESUMED breadcrumb and failed to continue to the next story in the loop

## Expected Behavior

Per sprint SKILL.md Phase 2 Step 2 ORCHESTRATOR_RESUME block:
> **MANDATORY CONTINUATION — DO NOT STOP HERE.** The Skill tool call above has returned a result. That result is a STATUS line from a nested skill — it is NOT a signal for you to stop. You have more stories to process in this layer. Continue to the next story in the layer loop.

The orchestrator should automatically continue after each Skill tool invocation returns STATUS:complete.

## Contributing Factors

1. Context window compaction occurred mid-sprint (after implementation-plan for 06c3-d6c7), causing a fresh session start for 5250-e85b
2. Prior to compaction, 3 separate manual user invocations were needed (b4c1-06cf, 147a-5eec, 06c3-d6c7)
3. The ORCHESTRATOR_RESUME blocks in SKILL.md exist specifically to prevent this (known bug 7d7a-b707) but were not preventing the stop

## Side Effects

Due to manual invocations, implementation-plan tasks were created without `--parent <story-id>` flag (the sprint orchestrator was not running them in the correct context), leaving 27 tasks orphaned (parent_id=null). This required a one-time EDIT event batch to fix parent_id for all affected tasks.
